reasoning: The forecast horizon is extremely short (barely over two months to the end of 2025) and there is no evidence of a dramatic doubling in the reported US annual death toll from AMR. The CDC continues to cite the figure of "more than 35,000" in its 2024 and 2025 updates based on the 2019 AR Threats Report. Furthermore, recent massive global modeling studies from GRAM and The Lancet (published late 2024) place the global annual death toll around 1.14-1.91 million. Pro-rating this to the US does not produce numbers anywhere near 70,000, and a 2023 presentation similarly refers broadly to "tens of thousands" of US deaths. The absence of any impending report or methodological shift that would double the official estimates justifies a very low probability. Setting p to the lowest robustly low limit (0.02).
